arts.ttl: Arsenal and Chelsea move for €100m Brazilian wonderkid
arts.sum: Arsenal and Chelsea are among the clubs to have enquired about Vitor Reis again in recent days, according to Brazilian media. According to the Brazilian edition of GOAL, Arsenal, Chelsea, Real Madrid and Inter have all made new contact with Palmeiras 'in recent days' to gather information on Reis.

arts.ttl: Liverpool star Trent Alexander-Arnold sends title race message amid Arsenal and Chelsea hope
arts.sum: "I think maybe they are still finding their feet in that first three or four months of the season and then after Christmas, into the New Year, you can forget about beating them because not many teams do. "Of course I think it was a big surprise to everyone," Alexander-Arnold explained when asked if he was shocked by City's start to the season.

arts.ttl: Coventry City 2-1 Hull City: Frank Lampard picks up first home win of his Sky Blues tenure - as Chelsea icon leads his side to comeback win against struggling Tigers
arts.sum: HENRY CLARK AT THE COVENTRY BUILDING SOCIETY ARENA: Victory over the Tigers was the Chelsea legend's first at home since being appointed manager at the end of November.

arts.ttl: Chelsea 'best team in Premier League' - Frank
arts.sum: Chelsea are "maybe the best team in the Premier League right now", says Brentford manager Thomas Frank.

arts.ttl: Chelsea set sights on signing ‘unbelievable’ £100m attacker, they’re really impressed by his development

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta reveals why Raheem Sterling has struggled for playing time as Arsenal boss insists it is NOT down to 'tactical reasons'
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has insisted that Raheem Sterling's sporadic playing time at Arsenal is not due to tactical reasons.

arts.ttl: Filip Jorgensen decision made as £58m ace drops out: Chelsea predicted starting XI v Brentford

arts.ttl: Jadon Sancho’s joy returns as his talent finds space to flourish at Chelsea
arts.sum: Winger’s move to London looks a smart piece of business now, with Manchester United dropping the ball again A penny for Manchester United’s thoughts when a day that began with Dan Ashworth’s ignominious departure from Old Trafford ended with their discarded £73m winger, Jadon Sancho, sparking Chelsea’s thrilling comeback at Tottenham last Sunday. Dysfunction has rarely operated on such a grand scale. United are a black hole, a place where young talent goes to disappear, but for Chelsea the opposite is true. Talent finding space to flourish at Stamford Bridge is not an issue any more. The development of Enzo Maresca’s side has moved at frightening speed and, after his struggles on and off the pitch at United, it has been fascinating to watch the joy return to Sancho’s game since joining Chelsea on loan in the summer. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Brentford man tempted to join Tottenham Hotspur
arts.sum: Tottenham manager Ange Postecoglou has endured a difficult season at the club. Spurs are 11th in the Premier League standings and their form this season has been highly disappointing.
